source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/RatingCurve/RatingCurveAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- X12 structural oracle. RatingCurveAnalysis drives a seeded DEMCzs MCMC over a single-segment BaRatin RatingCurve then builds the predicted-discharge mode/mean curve + credible band over 20 Stratify stage bins. curve_length == 20 (mode_curve length == stage_bins). The mode curve is deterministic Predict at the MAP, but the MAP itself comes from the seeded chain. The seeded DEMCzs posterior is CHAOTICALLY sensitive across the C# and C++ MersenneTwister streams: the deterministic model log-likelihood + MLE match C#/C++ to rel 1e-8 (proven by the model_estimation fixtures), but the 300-iteration seeded chain MAP diverges at ~1e-6 (model-density ULP differences amplified over the chain -- inherent chaotic sensitivity, NOT a port bug; see docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md). Per the chaotic-sensitivity rule the posterior-dependent curve is NOT pinned (never oracle_skip-masked, never a loosened tolerance); the fixture asserts the deterministic structural invariants that reproduce bit-identically across C#/C++/R/Python. Emitter-verified against the real RMC.BestFit class via tools/oracle_emitter.
